title: FG Ricci equations in normal form
summary: Defines the symbolic right-hand sides of the normal-form ambient Ricci equations and exposes them through an explicit Ricci component certificate.

mathematicalContent.heading: Normal-form Ricci equations
mathematicalContent.body: Let a prime denote ∂_ρ and let Ric(g_ρ) and ∇ refer to the slice metric. The file defines the standard base–base, base–∞, and ∞–∞ expressions assembled from g_ρ, its first two ρ-derivatives, and the slice Ricci tensor.

mathematicalContent.heading: Certificate boundary
mathematicalContent.body: RicciNormalFormCertificate stores an arbitrary ambient Ricci component function together with proofs that it equals the displayed symbolic expressions. The later theorems merely project those fields; this file does not contract a Riemann tensor or derive Ricci from the Christoffel table.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This structure extends metric and inverse component data by n, g′, g″, the slice Ricci tensor, and a formal covariant derivative ∇g′.
correspondence.leanType: structure RicciNormalFormComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalInverseMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: tracePrime is the trace of the first ρ-derivative g′ with respect to the proposed inverse g⁻¹.
traceDoublePrime is the corresponding trace of g″.
This is the quadratic two-tensor obtained by contracting two copies of g′ with one inverse metric.
correspondence.leanType: def quadraticPrime (C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι) (i j : ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This scalar contracts two copies of g′ with two inverse metrics.
This is the symbolic base–base right-hand side for the ambient Ricci tensor in straight normal form.
correspondence.leanType: def FG_RicciEq_ij (C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι) (i j : ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is the symbolic base–transverse right-hand side of the normal-form Ricci equation.
correspondence.leanType: def FG_RicciEq_iInf (C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι) (i : ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is the symbolic transverse–transverse right-hand side of the normal-form Ricci equation.
A certificate supplies ambient Ricci components and proofs that the 0I and I0 components vanish and that the base–base, base–∞, ∞–base, and ∞–∞ components equal the three defined FG expressions.
A RicciNormalFormCertificate implies that every component with first index 0 vanishes.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ricci_zero_left_normal_form [Fintype ι] {C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι} (H : RicciNormalFormCertificate C) (I : AmbientIndex ι) : H.Ricci AmbientIndex.zero I = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The certificate identifies each base–base ambient Ricci component with the defined expression ℛᵢⱼ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ricci_base_base_normal_form [Fintype ι] {C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι} (H : RicciNormalFormCertificate C) (i j : ι) : H.Ricci (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.FG_RicciEq_ij i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The certificate identifies each base–transverse component with ℛᵢ∞.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ricci_base_inf_normal_form [Fintype ι] {C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι} (H : RicciNormalFormCertificate C) (i : ι) : H.Ricci (AmbientIndex.base i) AmbientIndex.inf = C.FG_RicciEq_iInf i

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The certificate identifies the transverse–transverse component with ℛ∞∞.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ricci_inf_inf_normal_form [Fintype ι] {C : RicciNormalFormComponents ι} (H : RicciNormalFormCertificate C) : H.Ricci AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = C.FG_RicciEq_infInf

references.locator: Chapter 3, Ricci tensor of a straight ambient metric in normal form
references.note: Source for the three displayed normal-form Ricci component equations.

limitations: The component expressions are defined symbolically and related to a supplied Ricci function by certificate fields. The file does not derive Ricci from Christoffel symbols, verify that gInv is an inverse, or prove the certificate for a concrete ambient metric.
